findings.problem: The artifact pack still leaves the motor direct-match readiness contract unresolved. The proposal and delta spec require board-dependent adapters to validate the real board resource before claiming readiness, but the design stops at propagating later write failures and explicitly leaves open whether motor initialization needs a non-actuating capability probe or whether apply/disable failure reporting is sufficient. That leaves one of the targeted hidden-success bug classes under-specified for implementation.
findings.evidence: openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/proposal.md:7-11 requires truthful board-resource validation and failure propagation; openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/specs/tc264-to-true-ls2k0300-adapter-layer/spec.md:3-12 and :21-23 require truthful direct-match discovery and surfaced motor failures; openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/design.md:71-72, :173-176, and :301-302 leave the motor readiness/probe policy open; openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/tasks.md:9-12 does not resolve that choice.
findings.recommendation: Resolve the accepted motor readiness policy in the design before apply. State whether direct-match motor readiness is established by a non-actuating GPIO/PWM capability probe, by deterministic resource-existence checks before `ready=true`, or by keeping the motor path not-ready until a validated command path succeeds. Then update tasks and verification hooks to assert that exact contract.

findings.problem: The tasks do not require a failing-path board-smoke run that proves `run_remote_smoke.sh` now returns non-zero while still copying diagnostics. A normal rerun can pass without demonstrating that the exact false-success bug was fixed, so the pack does not fully cover the tested smoke-helper failure class.
findings.evidence: openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/specs/true-ls2k0300-port-workspace/spec.md:6-12 requires remote runtime failure to stay a local failure while log retrieval remains best-effort; openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/design.md:213-219 says implementation verification must demonstrate one board-side failing run that still leaves a local smoke log; openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/tasks.md:16-18 only asks for a rebuild, rerun, and generic evidence capture, with no explicit failing-run requirement.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit task before implementation verification to execute a controlled failing startup path or equivalent fault injection, record the local non-zero exit from `new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh`, and preserve the copied runtime log alongside the command-resolution evidence. Keep the existing normal rerun task separately if a passing smoke run is also desired.
